key_insight: eCNNTO uses an element-wise residual CNN to predict near-optimal densities from history, skipping most TO iterations while preserving spatial continuity.
engineering_relevance: It can substantially accelerate density-based topology optimization for high-resolution 2D/3D designs across varying loads, boundaries, geometries, and mesh sizes.
method: Train an element-based CNN with residual connections to map density histories to element density updates.
Use final-stage density histories as training data instead of early-stage histories to improve efficiency and reduce dataset needs.
Leverage spatial correlations between neighboring elements to avoid disconnected optimized features.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation on multiple 2D and 3D examples, with up to 90% iteration reduction in 2D and 97% in 3D.
watch_item: Generalization is promising, but performance may depend on the representativeness of the small training set and careful handling of different domain/mesh configurations.
paper_title: eCNNTO: A Highly Generalizable ConvNet for Accelerating Topology Optimization
